title: Bus encoding using metadata
abstract: Various additional and alternative aspects are described herein. In some aspects, the present disclosure provides a method of communicating data between an electronic unit (EU) of a system-on-chip (SoC) and a dynamic random access memory (DRAM). The method includes compressing data at the EU. The method further includes encoding the compressed data at the EU. The method further includes generating control data for decoding the encoded data at the EU. The method further includes generating metadata corresponding to the compressed data at the EU. The metadata further includes the control data. The method further includes directing storage of the encoded data and the metadata in the DRAM.
claims:
Claims 1. A method of communicating data between an electronic unit (EU) of a system- on-chip (SoC) and a dynamic random access memory (DRAM), the method comprising: compressing data at the EU; encoding the compressed data at the EU; generating control data for decoding the encoded data at the EU; generating metadata corresponding to the compressed data at the EU, the metadata further including the control data; and directing storage of the encoded data and the metadata in the DRAM. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the control data comprises one or more of an indication of whether bus inversion has been applied to the compressed data and a type of encoding applied to the compressed data. 3. The method of claim 1, further comprising: directing reading of the encoded data and the metadata from the DRAM, wherein the encoded data remains encoded during transfer between the EU and the DRAM. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein encoding the compressed data comprises minimizing a hamming distance between bits of the compressed data. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein directing storage of the encoded data in the DRAM comprises directing a write transaction of the encoded data to the DRAM, the write transaction comprising a plurality of beats, and wherein the control data indicates whether each beat of the plurality of beats is encoded or not. 6. The method of claim 1, wherein directing storage of the encoded data comprises: converting encoded data to a bus transaction; and sending the bus transaction to a system memory management unit (SMMU) over a bus interface, wherein the SMMU interfaces with a DRAM controller over the bus 

 interface, and wherein the DRAM controller interfaces with the DRAM over the bus interface. 7. An electronic unit (EU) of a system-on-chip (SoC) comprising: a bandwidth compression component configured to compress data; a bus encoding component configured to encode the compressed data and generate control data for decoding the encoded data; and a bus protocol conversion component configured to: generate metadata corresponding to the compressed data, the metadata further including the control data; and direct storage of the encoded data and the metadata in a dynamic random access memory (DRAM). 8. The EU of claim 7, wherein the control data comprises one or more of an indication of whether bus inversion has been applied to the compressed data and a type of encoding applied to the compressed data. 9. The EU of claim 7, wherein the bus protocol conversion component is further configured to: direct reading of the encoded data and the metadata from the DRAM, wherein the encoded data remains encoded during transfer between the EU and the DRAM. 10. The EU of claim 7, wherein to encode the compressed data comprises to minimize a hamming distance between bits of the compressed data. 11. The EU of claim 7, wherein to direct storage of the encoded data in the DRAM comprises to direct a write transaction of the encoded data to the DRAM, the write transaction comprising a plurality of beats, and wherein the control data indicates whether each beat of the plurality of beats is encoded or not. 12. The EU of claim 7, wherein to direct storage of the encoded data comprises to: convert encoded data to a bus transaction; and 

 send the bus transaction to a system memory management unit (SMMU) over a bus interface, wherein the SMMU interfaces with a DRAM controller over the bus interface, and wherein the DRAM controller interfaces with the DRAM over the bus interface. 13. An electronic unit (EU) of a system-on-chip (SoC) comprising: means for compressing data at the EU; means for encoding the compressed data at the EU; means for generating control data for decoding the encoded data at the EU; means for generating metadata corresponding to the compressed data at the EU, the metadata further including the control data; and means for directing storage of the encoded data and the metadata in the DRAM. 14. The EU of claim 13, wherein the control data comprises one or more of an indication of whether bus inversion has been applied to the compressed data and a type of encoding applied to the compressed data. 15. The EU of claim 13, further comprising: means for directing reading of the encoded data and the metadata from the DRAM, wherein the encoded data remains encoded during transfer between the EU and the DRAM. 16. The EU of claim 13, wherein means for encoding the compressed data comprises means for minimizing a hamming distance between bits of the compressed data. 17. The EU of claim 13, wherein means for directing storage of the encoded data in the DRAM comprises means for directing a write transaction of the encoded data to the DRAM, the write transaction comprising a plurality of beats, and wherein the control data indicates whether each beat of the plurality of beats is encoded or not. 18. The EU of claim 13, wherein means for directing storage of the encoded data comprises: 

 means for converting encoded data to a bus transaction; and means for sending the bus transaction to a system memory management unit (SMMU) over a bus interface, wherein the SMMU interfaces with a DRAM controller over the bus interface, and wherein the DRAM controller interfaces with the DRAM over the bus interface. 19. A computer readable medium having instructions stored thereon for causing an electronic unit (EU) of a system-on-chip (SoC) to perform a method of communicating data between the EU and a dynamic random access memory (DRAM), the method comprising: compressing data at the EU; encoding the compressed data at the EU; generating control data for decoding the encoded data at the EU; generating metadata corresponding to the compressed data at the EU, the metadata further including the control data; and directing storage of the encoded data and the metadata in the DRAM. 20. The computer readable medium of claim 19, wherein the control data comprises one or more of an indication of whether bus inversion has been applied to the compressed data and a type of encoding applied to the compressed data. 21. The computer readable medium of claim 19, wherein the method further comprises: directing reading of the encoded data and the metadata from the DRAM, wherein the encoded data remains encoded during transfer between the EU and the DRAM. 22. The computer readable medium of claim 19, wherein encoding the compressed data comprises minimizing a hamming distance between bits of the compressed data. 23. The computer readable medium of claim 19, wherein directing storage of the encoded data in the DRAM comprises directing a write transaction of the encoded data 

 to the DRAM, the write transaction comprising a plurality of beats, and wherein the control data indicates whether each beat of the plurality of beats is encoded or not. 24. The computer readable medium of claim 19, wherein directing storage of the encoded data comprises: converting encoded data to a bus transaction; and sending the bus transaction to a system memory management unit (SMMU) over a bus interface, wherein the SMMU interfaces with a DRAM controller over the bus interface, and wherein the DRAM controller interfaces with the DRAM over the bus interface.
